title: papaver somniferum var. apodocarpum
intro: Papaver somniferum var. apodocarpum, commonly known as the opium poppy, is a variant of the opium poppy plant, a species in the poppy family (Papaveraceae) known for its delicate flowers and medicinal properties. The plant is rich in alkaloids, including morphine, codeine, and thebaine, which possess analgesic, antitussive, and antidiarrheal properties, making it a valuable source of medicines. The health benefits of Papaver somniferum var. apodocarpum include pain relief, cough suppression, and treatment of diarrhea, as well as its potential use in managing opioid addiction. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by extracting its alkaloids from the latex or seeds, which can be used to produce medications such as morphine and codeine, or taken as a tincture or capsule for its medicinal properties.
